title: anagallis arvensis var. repens
intro: Anagallis arvensis var. repens, also known as creeping bogbean or creeping anagallis, is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the Primulaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its expectorant and antispasmodic effects, which have been found to help in relieving coughs and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Anagallis arvensis var. repens include its ability to act as an antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and digestive problems. Herbal preparations of Anagallis arvensis var. repens include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies.
